justification: O(n) two-Set algorithm is correct and well-explained. AC4 edge case (3+ occurrences) is tested. All 5 ACs are mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence section does not explicitly note that AC3 (performance) requires runtime validation rather than a unit test — it only mentions CI flakiness. Additionally, while the approach section explains why O(n) was chosen, it does not explicitly state that O(n²) was rejected due to AC3; it frames the choice positively rather than noting the rejection of the alternative.

observations.text: O(n) two-Set algorithm is clean and correct — avoids Map overhead while preserving deduplication via Set semantics
AC4 edge case explicitly tested with IDs appearing 3+ times, returning once
All 5 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage with clear test references
Performance test included with thoughtful threshold justification accounting for Jest overhead
Deviations section transparently documents the 200ms threshold choice
Confidence section does not note that AC3 is fundamentally a runtime characteristic that cannot be verified by a unit test — only mentions CI flakiness
Does not explicitly state that O(n²) was rejected due to AC3; the explanation is framed as a positive choice rather than a deliberate rejection of the alternative
